### CursorHookProvider

typeof CursorHookProvider

Hook provider for Cursor.  Cursor registers hooks via its config system at `.cursor/hooks.json`. Supported handler types: command, prompt.  CAAMP 1.9.1 reveals Cursor supports 10 of 16 canonical events. Previously this provider was a no-op stub. It now provides full event mapping and CAAMP normalizer integration.  Event mapping is based on `getProviderHookProfile('cursor')` from CAAMP 1.9.1. Async accessors (`getSupportedCanonicalEvents`, `getProviderProfile`) call CAAMP directly when available.  Since hooks are registered through the config system (managed by the install provider), `registerNativeHooks` and `unregisterNativeHooks` track registration state without performing filesystem operations.

Members:

#### registered

boolean

Whether hooks have been registered for the current session.

#### mapProviderEvent()

(providerEvent: string) => string | null

Map a Cursor native event name to a CAAMP canonical hook event name.  Looks up the native event name in the map derived from `getProviderHookProfile('cursor').mappings` (CAAMP 1.9.1). Cursor uses camelCase names (e.g. "preToolUse", "sessionStart").  Returns null for unsupported events (PermissionRequest, PreModel, PostModel, PostCompact, Notification, ConfigChange).

Parameters:
- providerEvent: Cursor native event name (e.g. "preToolUse", "sessionStart")

Returns: CAAMP canonical event name, or null if unmapped  T165

#### registerNativeHooks()

(_projectDir: string) => Promise<void>

Register native hooks for a project.  For Cursor, hooks are registered via the config system (`.cursor/hooks.json`), managed by the install provider. This method marks hooks as registered without performing filesystem operations.  Iterating supported events is handled at install time using `getSupportedCanonicalEvents()` to enumerate all 10 supported hooks.

Parameters:
- _projectDir: Project directory (unused; Cursor config manages registration)  T165

#### unregisterNativeHooks()

() => Promise<void>

Unregister native hooks.  For Cursor, this is a no-op since hooks are managed through the config system. Unregistration happens via the install provider's uninstall method.   T165

#### isRegistered()

() => boolean

Check whether hooks have been registered via `registerNativeHooks`.

#### getEventMap()

() => Readonly<Record<string, string>>

Get the native→canonical event mapping for introspection and debugging.  Returns the map derived from `getProviderHookProfile('cursor').mappings` (CAAMP 1.9.1). Use `getSupportedCanonicalEvents()` to enumerate canonical names via live CAAMP APIs.

Returns: Immutable record of native event name → canonical event name

#### getSupportedCanonicalEvents()

() => Promise<string[]>

Enumerate supported canonical events via CAAMP's `getSupportedEvents()`.  Calls `getSupportedEvents('cursor')` from the CAAMP normalizer to get the authoritative list. Cursor supports 10 of 16 canonical events. Falls back to the values of the static event map when CAAMP is unavailable at runtime.

Returns: Array of CAAMP canonical event names supported by Cursor  T165

#### getProviderProfile()

() => Promise<unknown | null>

Retrieve the full provider hook profile from CAAMP.  Calls `getProviderHookProfile('cursor')` from the CAAMP normalizer to get the complete profile: hook system type (`config`), config path (`.cursor/hooks.json`), handler types (command, prompt), and all event mappings. Returns null when CAAMP is unavailable at runtime.

Returns: Provider hook profile or null if CAAMP is unavailable  T165

#### toNativeEvent()

(canonical: string) => Promise<string | null>

Translate a CAAMP canonical event to its Cursor native name via CAAMP.  Calls `toNative(canonical, 'cursor')` from the CAAMP normalizer. Returns null for unsupported events or when CAAMP is unavailable.

Parameters:
- canonical: CAAMP canonical event name (e.g. "PreToolUse")

Returns: Cursor native event name (e.g. "preToolUse") or null  T165

### CursorInstallProvider

typeof CursorInstallProvider

Install provider for Cursor.  Manages CLEO's integration with Cursor by: 1. Creating/updating .cursorrules with -references (legacy) 2. Creating .cursor/rules/cleo.mdc with -references (modern)

(projectDir: string) => boolean

Update instruction files with CLEO -references.  Handles both legacy (.cursorrules) and modern (.cursor/rules/cleo.mdc) formats.

Returns: true if any file was created or modified

#### updateLegacyRules()

(projectDir: string) => boolean

Update legacy .cursorrules file with -references. Only modifies the file if it already exists (does not create it).

Returns: true if the file was modified

#### updateModernRules()

(projectDir: string) => boolean

Create or update .cursor/rules/cleo.mdc with CLEO references.  MDC (Markdown Component) format is Cursor's modern rule file format. Each .mdc file in .cursor/rules/ is loaded as a rule set.

Returns: true if the file was created or modified

#### getUpdatedFileList()

(projectDir: string) => string[]

Get list of instruction files that were updated.

### OpenCodeSpawnProvider

typeof OpenCodeSpawnProvider

Spawn provider for OpenCode.  Spawns detached OpenCode CLI processes for subagent execution. Each spawn ensures a CLEO subagent definition exists, then runs `opencode run --format json --agent <name> --title <title> <prompt>` as a detached, unref'd child process.

Members:

#### processMap

Map<string, TrackedProcess>

Map of instance IDs to tracked process info.

#### canSpawn()

() => Promise<boolean>

Check if the OpenCode CLI is available in PATH.

Returns: true if `opencode` is found via `which`

#### spawn()

(context: SpawnContext) => Promise<SpawnResult>

Spawn a subagent via OpenCode CLI.  Ensures the CLEO subagent definition exists in the project's .opencode/agent/ directory, then spawns a detached OpenCode process. The process runs independently of the parent.

Parameters:
- context: Spawn context with taskId, prompt, and options

Returns: Spawn result with instance ID and status

#### listRunning()

() => Promise<SpawnResult[]>

List currently running OpenCode subagent processes.  Checks each tracked process via kill(pid, 0) to verify it is still alive. Dead processes are automatically cleaned from the tracking map.

Returns: Array of spawn results for running processes

#### terminate()

(instanceId: string) => Promise<void>

Terminate a running spawn by instance ID.  Sends SIGTERM to the tracked process. If the process is not found or has already exited, this is a no-op.

Parameters:
- instanceId: ID of the spawn instance to terminate

### CursorSpawnProvider

typeof CursorSpawnProvider

() => Promise<boolean>

Check if Cursor supports spawning subagents.

Returns: false (Cursor does not support CLI spawning)

#### spawn()

(_context: SpawnContext) => Promise<SpawnResult>

Attempt to spawn a subagent via Cursor.  Always throws because Cursor does not support subagent spawning. Callers should check canSpawn() before calling this method.

- _context: Unused; spawning is not supported

- _instanceId: Unused; no processes to terminate

### LAFSCommandError

string

#### category

#### recoverable

boolean

Whether the operation can be retried after fixing the root cause.

#### suggestion

string

Human-readable suggestion for resolving the error.

#### retryAfterMs

number | null

Optional delay in milliseconds before retrying, or null.

#### details

unknown

Optional additional error details payload.

string

#### status

number | undefined

The marketplace identifier used in search results.

#### search()

(query: string, limit?: number) => Promise<MarketplaceResult[]>

Search for skills by query string.

Parameters:
- query: Search query to match against skill names and descriptions.
- limit: Maximum number of results to return.

Returns: Array of marketplace results sorted by stars.

#### getSkill()

(scopedName: string) => Promise<MarketplaceResult | null>

Look up a specific skill by its scoped name.

Parameters:
- scopedName: The scoped skill name (e.g. `"@author/skill-name"`).

Returns: The matching marketplace result, or `null` if not found.

### MarketplaceClient

typeof MarketplaceClient

Unified marketplace client that aggregates results from multiple marketplace adapters.  Queries all configured marketplaces in parallel, deduplicates results by scoped name, and sorts by star count.

Example:
  const client = new MarketplaceClient();
  const results = await client.search("filesystem");
  for (const r of results) {
    console.log(`${r.scopedName} (${r.stars} stars)`);
  }

Members:

#### adapters

MarketplaceAdapter[]

Configured marketplace adapters.

#### search()

(query: string, limit?: number) => Promise<MarketplaceResult[]>

Search all marketplaces and return deduplicated, sorted results.  Queries all adapters in parallel and deduplicates by `scopedName`, keeping the entry with the highest star count. Results are sorted by stars descending.

Parameters:
- query: Search query string
- limit: Maximum number of results to return (default: 20)

Returns: Deduplicated and sorted marketplace results

Example:
  const results = await client.search("code review", 10);

#### getSkill()

(scopedName: string) => Promise<MarketplaceResult | null>

Get a specific skill by its scoped name from any marketplace.  Tries each adapter in order and returns the first match.

Parameters:
- scopedName: Scoped skill name (e.g. `"@author/my-skill"`)

Returns: The marketplace result, or `null` if not found in any marketplace

Example:
  const skill = await client.getSkill("@anthropic/memory");

### CleoError

typeof CleoError

Structured error class for CLEO operations. Carries an exit code, human-readable message, and optional fix suggestions. Produces LAFS-conformant error shapes via toLAFSError() and RFC 9457 Problem Details via toProblemDetails().

Members:

#### code

ExitCode

#### fix

string | undefined

#### alternatives

{ action: string; command: string; }[] | undefined

#### toLAFSError()

() => LAFSError

Produce a LAFS-conformant error object.   T4655

#### toProblemDetails()

() => ProblemDetails

Produce an RFC 9457 Problem Details object.   T5240

#### toJSON()

() => Record<string, unknown>

Structured JSON representation for LAFS output (backward compatible).

#### getHttpStatus()

() => number

Derive HTTP status from exit code range. Used as fallback when catalog lookup misses.

### HookRegistry

typeof HookRegistry

Central registry for hook handlers.  Manages registration, priority-based ordering, and async dispatch of hook handlers. Provides best-effort execution where errors in one handler do not block others.  Backward compatibility: handlers registered with legacy `on`-prefix event names (e.g. `onSessionStart`) are automatically remapped to their canonical equivalents (e.g. `SessionStart`) with a deprecation warning.

Members:

#### handlers

Map<HookEvent, HookRegistration<HookPayload>[]>

#### config

HookConfig

#### resolveEvent()

(event: string) => HookEvent

Resolve a potentially-legacy event name to its canonical equivalent.  If the event name matches a known legacy `on`-prefix name, it is remapped and a deprecation warning is logged. Unknown names pass through unchanged so callers using the new canonical names are unaffected.

#### register()

<T extends HookPayload>(registration: HookRegistration<T>) => () => void

Register a hook handler for a specific event.  Handlers are sorted by priority (highest first) and executed in parallel when the event is dispatched.  Backward compatibility: legacy `on`-prefix event names are automatically remapped to their canonical equivalents.

Parameters:
- registration: The hook registration containing event, handler, priority, and ID

Returns: A function to unregister the handler

Example:
  const unregister = hooks.register({
    id: 'my-handler',
    event: 'SessionStart',
    handler: async (root, payload) => { console.log('Session started'); },
    priority: 100
  });
  
  // Later: unregister()

#### dispatch()

<T extends HookPayload>(event: HookEvent, projectRoot: string, payload: T) => Promise<void>

Dispatch an event to all registered handlers.  Executes handlers in parallel using Promise.allSettled for best-effort execution. Errors in individual handlers are logged but do not block other handlers or propagate to the caller.  Backward compatibility: legacy `on`-prefix event names are automatically remapped to their canonical equivalents.

Parameters:
- event: The CAAMP canonical hook event to dispatch
- projectRoot: The project root directory path
- payload: The event payload (typed by event)

Returns: Promise that resolves when all handlers have completed

Example:
  await hooks.dispatch('SessionStart', '/project', {
    timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
    sessionId: 'sess-123',
    name: 'My Session',
    scope: 'feature'
  });

#### isEnabled()

(event: HookEvent) => boolean

Check if a specific event is currently enabled.  Both the global enabled flag and the per-event flag must be true. Automatically resolves legacy `on`-prefix event names.

Parameters:
- event: The CAAMP hook event to check

Returns: True if the event is enabled

#### setConfig()

(config: Partial<HookConfig>) => void

Update the hook system configuration.  Merges the provided config with the existing config.

Parameters:
- config: Partial configuration to apply

Example:
  hooks.setConfig({ enabled: false }); // Disable all hooks
  hooks.setConfig({ events: { PostToolUseFailure: false } }); // Disable specific event

#### getConfig()

() => HookConfig

Get the current hook configuration.

Returns: A copy of the current configuration

#### listHandlers()

(event: HookEvent) => HookRegistration[]

List all registered handlers for a specific event.  Returns handlers in priority order (highest first). Automatically resolves legacy `on`-prefix event names.

Parameters:
- event: The CAAMP hook event

Returns: Array of hook registrations

### SafetyDataAccessor

typeof SafetyDataAccessor

Safety-enabled DataAccessor wrapper.  Wraps any DataAccessor implementation and automatically applies safety checks to all write operations. Read operations pass through.  This class CANNOT be bypassed - it's the only way to get a DataAccessor from the factory (unless emergency disable is active).

### LocalEmbeddingProvider

typeof LocalEmbeddingProvider

Local embedding provider backed by xenova/transformers.  Produces 384-dimension Float32Array vectors compatible with the brain_embeddings vec0 table. The model is downloaded on first use and cached locally by the transformers library.  Use `initDefaultProvider` (in brain-embedding.ts) to register an instance when brain.embedding.enabled=true and brain.embedding.provider='local'.

Members:

#### dimensions

384

Number of dimensions produced — must match brain_embeddings vec0 table.

#### isAvailable()

() => boolean

Whether the pipeline has been successfully initialized and is ready to produce embeddings.

#### embed()

(text: string) => Promise<Float32Array>

Convert a single text string into a 384-dimension float vector. Triggers model download on first call if not already cached.

Parameters:
- text: The text to embed.

Returns: A Float32Array of length 384.

#### embedBatch()

(texts: string[]) => Promise<Float32Array[]>

Embed multiple texts in sequence, reusing the cached pipeline.

Parameters:
- texts: Array of text strings to embed.

Returns: Array of Float32Array vectors, one per input text.

### AdapterManager

typeof AdapterManager

Central adapter manager. Singleton per process.  Lifecycle:   1. discover() — scan for adapter packages and their manifests   2. activate(id) — load, initialize, and set as active adapter   3. getActive() — return the current active adapter   4. dispose() — clean up all initialized adapters

Members:

#### instance

AdapterManager | null

#### adapters

Map<string, CLEOProviderAdapter>

#### manifests

Map<string, AdapterManifest>

#### hookCleanups

Map<string, (() => void)[]>

string | null

#### projectRoot

string

#### getInstance()

(projectRoot: string) => AdapterManager

#### resetInstance()

() => void

Reset singleton (for testing).

#### discover()

() => AdapterManifest[]

Discover adapter manifests from packages/adapters/. Returns manifests found (does not load adapter code yet).

#### detectActive()

() => string[]

Auto-detect which adapters match the current environment and return their manifest IDs.

#### activate()

(adapterId: string) => Promise<CLEOProviderAdapter>

Load and initialize an adapter by manifest ID. Dynamically imports from the manifest's packagePath — no hardcoded adapters.

#### getActive()

() => CLEOProviderAdapter | null

Get the currently active adapter, or null if none.

#### getActiveId()

() => string | null

Get the active adapter's ID, or null.

#### get()

(adapterId: string) => CLEOProviderAdapter | null

Get a specific adapter by ID.

#### getManifest()

(adapterId: string) => AdapterManifest | null

Get the manifest for a specific adapter.

#### listAdapters()

List all known adapters with summary info.

#### healthCheckAll()

() => Promise<Map<string, AdapterHealthStatus>>

Run health check on all initialized adapters.

#### healthCheck()

(adapterId: string) => Promise<AdapterHealthStatus>

Health check a single adapter.

#### dispose()

() => Promise<void>

Dispose all initialized adapters.

#### disposeAdapter()

(adapterId: string) => Promise<void>

Dispose a single adapter.

#### wireAdapterHooks()

(adapterId: string, adapter: CLEOProviderAdapter) => Promise<void>

Wire an adapter's hook event map into CLEO's HookRegistry. Creates bridging handlers at priority 50 for each mapped event.

#### cleanupAdapterHooks()

(adapterId: string, adapter: CLEOProviderAdapter) => Promise<void>

### LocalTransport

typeof LocalTransport

In-process SQLite transport for fully offline agent messaging.

Members:

#### name

"local"

#### state

LocalTransportState | null

#### connect()

(config: TransportConnectConfig) => Promise<void>

Connect to signaldock.db for in-process messaging.  Opens the database, sets WAL mode pragmas, and verifies the messages table exists. Throws if signaldock.db is missing or uninitialized (run `cleo init` first).

#### disconnect()

() => Promise<void>

Close the database connection and stop any subscriber polling.

#### push()

(to: string, content: string, options?: { conversationId?: string; replyTo?: string; }) => Promise<{ messageId: string; }>

Store a message in signaldock.db.  Inserts into the messages table with status 'pending'. For conversation messages, also links via conversation_participants if not already present.

#### poll()

(options?: { limit?: number; since?: string; }) => Promise<ConduitMessage[]>

Poll for messages addressed to this agent.  Returns messages with status 'pending' where to_agent_id matches the connected agent. Messages are returned oldest-first.

#### ack()

(messageIds: string[]) => Promise<void>

Acknowledge messages by marking them as 'delivered'.  Updates the status and delivered_at timestamp for each message ID.

#### subscribe()

(handler: (message: ConduitMessage) => void) => () => void

Subscribe to real-time local messages.  Since this is in-process, subscribers are notified synchronously when push() is called. Additionally, a polling interval checks for messages inserted by other processes (e.g., Rust CLI).

Returns: Unsubscribe function.

#### isAvailable()

(cwd?: string) => boolean

Check whether signaldock.db is available for local transport.  Used by factory.ts to decide whether to use LocalTransport.

#### pollAndNotify()

() => Promise<void>

Poll for new messages and notify subscribers (cross-process sync).

#### notifySubscribers()

(message: ConduitMessage) => void

Notify all active subscribers of a new message.

#### ensureDmConversation()

(fromAgentId: string, toAgentId: string) => string

Ensure a DM conversation exists between two agents.  Conversations store participants as a comma-separated TEXT field. We search for existing private conversations containing both agents.

Returns: The conversation ID.

#### ensureConnected()

() => void

Throw if not connected.

### SseTransport

typeof SseTransport

SseTransport — real-time SSE with HTTP polling fallback.

Members:

#### name

"sse"

#### state

SseTransportState | null

#### connect()

(config: TransportConnectConfig) => Promise<void>

Connect to the SSE endpoint for real-time message delivery.  If SSE connection fails, falls back to HTTP polling mode. Auth is conveyed via query parameter (SSE doesn't support custom headers).

#### disconnect()

() => Promise<void>

Disconnect the transport, closing SSE and clearing all state.

#### push()

(to: string, content: string, options?: { conversationId?: string; replyTo?: string; }) => Promise<{ messageId: string; }>

Send a message via HTTP POST.  SSE is receive-only — all sends go through HTTP regardless of SSE state.

#### poll()

(options?: { limit?: number; since?: string; }) => Promise<ConduitMessage[]>

Poll for messages.  In SSE mode: drains the internal message buffer (no HTTP request). In HTTP fallback mode: fetches via GET /messages/peek.

#### ack()

(messageIds: string[]) => Promise<void>

Acknowledge messages via HTTP POST.

#### subscribe()

(handler: (message: ConduitMessage) => void) => () => void

Subscribe to real-time messages.  In SSE mode, messages are pushed to the handler as they arrive. In HTTP fallback mode, polls on an interval.

#### connectSse()

() => Promise<void>

Establish SSE connection. Resolves when open, rejects on error.

#### handleSseMessage()

(event: MessageEvent) => void

Handle an incoming SSE message event.

#### handleSseDisconnect()

() => void

Handle SSE connection drop — attempt reconnect with backoff.

#### httpPoll()

(options?: { limit?: number; since?: string; }) => Promise<ConduitMessage[]>

HTTP poll for messages (used in fallback mode).

#### httpFetch()

(path: string, init: RequestInit) => Promise<Response>

Make an authenticated HTTP request to the API.

#### ensureConnected()

() => void

Throw if not connected.

### ApprovalManager

typeof ApprovalManager

Manages approval tokens for CANT workflow gates.  Tokens are stored in-memory per instance. In production, these are persisted via the session's `approvalTokensJson` column in tasks.db.

Members:

#### tokens

Map<string, ApprovalToken>

In-memory token store keyed by token UUID.

#### generateToken()

(sessionId: string, workflowName: string, gateName: string, message: string, workflowHash: string, requestedBy: string, expiresInMs?: number) => ApprovalToken

Generates a new approval token for a workflow gate.

Parameters:
- sessionId: The session that owns this token.
- workflowName: The workflow containing the approval gate.
- gateName: The label of the specific approval gate.
- message: The message to display to the approver.
- workflowHash: SHA-256 hash of the workflow definition.
- requestedBy: The agent/workflow requesting approval.
- expiresInMs: Token lifetime in milliseconds (default: 24h).

Returns: The generated approval token.

#### validateToken()

(tokenId: string, currentSessionId: string, currentWorkflowHash: string) => TokenValidation

Validates an approval token against the current execution context.  Checks: (a) token exists, (b) sessionId matches, (c) status is pending, (d) not expired, (e) workflowHash matches current workflow.

Parameters:
- tokenId: The token UUID to validate.
- currentSessionId: The current session to validate against.
- currentWorkflowHash: The current workflow hash for TOCTOU check.

Returns: Validation result with reason if invalid.

#### approveToken()

(tokenId: string, approvedBy: string) => boolean

Approves a pending token.  Performs an atomic CAS transition: `pending -> approved`. If the token is not in `pending` state, the operation is a no-op and returns false.

Parameters:
- tokenId: The token UUID to approve.
- approvedBy: The identifier of the approving actor.

Returns: `true` if the transition succeeded, `false` if CAS failed.

#### rejectToken()

(tokenId: string) => boolean

Rejects a pending token.  Performs an atomic CAS transition: `pending -> rejected`.

Parameters:
- tokenId: The token UUID to reject.

Returns: `true` if the transition succeeded, `false` if CAS failed.

#### expireToken()

(tokenId: string) => boolean

Expires a pending token.  Performs an atomic CAS transition: `pending -> expired`.

Parameters:
- tokenId: The token UUID to expire.

Returns: `true` if the transition succeeded, `false` if CAS failed.

#### getToken()

(tokenId: string) => ApprovalToken | undefined

Retrieves a token by its UUID.

Parameters:
- tokenId: The token UUID to look up.

Returns: The token if found, otherwise undefined.

#### getTokensForSession()

(sessionId: string) => ApprovalToken[]

Lists all tokens for a given session.

Parameters:
- sessionId: The session to filter by.

Returns: Array of tokens belonging to the session.

#### computeWorkflowHash()

(workflowText: string) => string

Computes a SHA-256 hash of a workflow definition string.  Used for TOCTOU protection: the hash at token creation time is compared against the hash at approval time.

Parameters:
- workflowText: The raw workflow definition text.

Returns: Hex-encoded SHA-256 hash.

#### transitionStatus()

(tokenId: string, targetStatus: ApprovalTokenStatus) => boolean

Atomically transitions a token from `pending` to the target status.

Parameters:
- tokenId: The token UUID.
- targetStatus: The desired new status.

Returns: `true` if the CAS transition succeeded.

### DefaultDiscretionEvaluator

typeof DefaultDiscretionEvaluator

Default discretion evaluator that always returns `true`.  This is a stub implementation. The real LLM-backed evaluator will be implemented as a separate task. In production, this should be replaced with an evaluator that: - Calls the LLM API with the condition in a structured field - Uses structured prompting (tool use / JSON mode) - Returns a boolean judgment

Members:

#### evaluate()

(_condition: string, _context: DiscretionContext) => Promise<boolean>

Always returns `true` (stub).

Members:

#### evaluationLog

#### setResponse()

Evaluate using the configured response map.

#### getLog()

() => ReadonlyArray<{ condition: string; context: DiscretionContext; result: boolean; }>

Get the log of all evaluations performed.

#### clearLog()

() => void

Reset the evaluation log.

### RateLimitedDiscretionEvaluator

typeof RateLimitedDiscretionEvaluator

Wraps a discretion evaluator with rate limiting.  Enforces a configurable maximum number of discretion evaluations per workflow execution. Default limit is 100 per the spec. This prevents runaway costs from loops containing discretion conditions.

Members:

#### evaluationCount

number

#### evaluate()

(condition: string, context: DiscretionContext) => Promise<boolean>

Evaluate with rate limiting.

#### getEvaluationCount()

() => number

Get the current evaluation count.

#### resetCount()

() => void

Reset the evaluation counter (for reuse across workflow runs).

### WorkflowExecutor

typeof WorkflowExecutor

Executes CANT workflow definitions.  The executor processes each statement in the workflow body sequentially, maintaining an execution scope for variable bindings and dispatching to the appropriate handler based on statement type.

#### approvalManager

- context: Parameter values and initial context.

Returns: Execution result with outputs and step results.

### EmbeddingQueue

typeof EmbeddingQueue

Singleton embedding queue.  Batches embedding requests and processes them via a worker thread, keeping heavy model inference off the main event loop.  Use `getEmbeddingQueue` to obtain the shared instance.

Members:

#### queue

QueueItem[]

#### worker

import("worker_threads").Worker | null

#### workerAvailable

boolean

#### draining

boolean

#### shutdownPromise

Promise<void> | null

#### callbacks

Map<string, (observationId: string, embedding: Float32Array) => Promise<void>>

Store the observation ID → DB write callback so the worker result can be persisted back to brain_embeddings without coupling to SQLite here.

#### initWorker()

() => Promise<void>

Initialise the worker thread if possible. Called lazily on first enqueue.

#### enqueue()

(observationId: string, text: string, onComplete: (observationId: string, embedding: Float32Array) => Promise<void>) => void

Add an observation to the embedding queue.  The observation must already be persisted in brain_observations before calling this method. `onComplete` is called asynchronously with the generated vector once the worker finishes.

Parameters:
- observationId: The brain observation ID (e.g. `O-abc123-0`)
- text: Raw text to embed
- onComplete: Callback to persist the embedding vector

#### scheduleDrain()

() => void

Schedule the next drain cycle via setImmediate.

#### drain()

() => Promise<void>

Process up to `BATCH_SIZE` items from the queue. Uses the worker thread when available, falls back to inline setImmediate.

#### fallbackEmbed()

(item: QueueItem) => Promise<void>

Inline fallback embedding — used when worker thread is unavailable. Runs directly on the main thread (but inside setImmediate to yield first).

#### shutdown()

() => Promise<void>

Flush the queue and terminate the worker thread.  Waits for in-flight worker messages to drain, then terminates. Safe to call multiple times — subsequent calls return the same promise.

#### doShutdown()

() => Promise<void>

### TokenEstimator

typeof TokenEstimator

Character-based token estimator for JSON payloads.

Example:
  const estimator = new TokenEstimator({ charsPerToken: 4 });
  const tokens = estimator.estimate({ name: "hello", items: [1, 2, 3] });

Members:

#### options

Required<TokenEstimatorOptions>

Resolved configuration with defaults applied

#### estimate()

(value: unknown) => number

Estimate tokens for any JavaScript value. Handles circular references, nested objects, arrays, and Unicode.

Parameters:
- value: Any value to estimate

Returns: Estimated token count

#### estimateJSON()

(json: string) => number

Estimate tokens from a JSON string. More efficient if you already have the JSON string.

Parameters:
- json: JSON string to estimate

Returns: Estimated token count

#### estimateWithTracking()

(value: unknown, seen: WeakSet<object>, depth: number) => number

Internal recursive estimation with circular reference tracking.

Parameters:
- value: Value to estimate
- seen: WeakSet tracking visited objects for circular reference detection
- depth: Current recursion depth

Returns: Estimated token count for this value

#### estimateArray()

(arr: unknown[], seen: WeakSet<object>, depth: number) => number

Estimate tokens for an array.

Parameters:
- arr: Array to estimate
- seen: WeakSet tracking visited objects
- depth: Current recursion depth

Returns: Estimated token count including brackets and separators

#### estimateObject()

(obj: Record<string, unknown>, seen: WeakSet<object>, depth: number) => number

Estimate tokens for a plain object.

Parameters:
- obj: Object to estimate
- seen: WeakSet tracking visited objects
- depth: Current recursion depth

Returns: Estimated token count including braces, keys, colons, and separators

#### canSerialize()

(value: unknown) => boolean

Check if a value can be safely serialized (no circular refs).

Parameters:
- value: Value to check

Returns: `true` if `JSON.stringify` succeeds without throwing

#### safeStringify()

(value: unknown) => string

Serialize a value to JSON with circular reference handling.

Parameters:
- value: Value to serialize

Returns: JSON string with circular references replaced by `"[Circular]"`

<T>(value: T) => T

### LafsError

typeof LafsError

Error subclass that carries the full `LAFSError` payload.

  try {
    parseLafsResponse(envelope);
  } catch (err) {
    if (err instanceof LafsError) {
      console.log(err.code, err.agentAction);
    }
  }

#### code

string

#### category

LAFSErrorCategory

High-level classification of the error.

#### retryable

boolean

Whether the operation can be retried without modification.

#### retryAfterMs

number | null

boolean

boolean | undefined

Whether the error requires human or higher-privilege intervention.

string | undefined

### TaskManager

typeof TaskManager

In-memory task manager implementing A2A task lifecycle.

Members:

#### tasks

Map<string, Task>

Map of task ID to Task object

#### contextIndex

Map<string, Set<string>>

Index mapping context ID to set of task IDs

#### createTask()

(options?: CreateTaskOptions) => Task

Create a new task in the submitted state.

Parameters:
- options: Task creation options including context, metadata, and references

Returns: Deep clone of the newly created task

Example:
  const manager = new TaskManager();
  const task = manager.createTask({ contextId: 'ctx-1', metadata: { source: 'api' } });

#### createRefinedTask()

(referenceTaskIds: string[], options?: Omit<CreateTaskOptions, "referenceTaskIds">) => Task

Create a refinement/follow-up task referencing existing task(s).

Parameters:
- referenceTaskIds: IDs of parent tasks to reference
- options: Additional task creation options (excluding referenceTaskIds)

Returns: Deep clone of the newly created refinement task

Example:
  const refined = manager.createRefinedTask(['task-1'], { parallelFollowUp: true });

#### getTask()

(taskId: string) => Task

Get a task by ID.

Parameters:
- taskId: ID of the task to retrieve

Returns: Deep clone of the requested task

Example:
  const task = manager.getTask('some-task-id');
  console.log(task.status.state);

#### listTasks()

(options?: ListTasksOptions) => ListTasksResult

List tasks with optional filtering and pagination.

Parameters:
- options: Filtering and pagination options

Returns: Paginated result with tasks and optional next page token

Example:
  const result = manager.listTasks({ contextId: 'ctx-1', state: 'working', limit: 10 });
  for (const task of result.tasks) {
    console.log(task.id, task.status.state);
  }

#### updateTaskStatus()

(taskId: string, state: TaskState, message?: Message) => Task

Update task status. Enforces valid transitions and terminal state immutability.

Parameters:
- taskId: ID of the task to update
- state: New state to transition to
- message: Optional message to attach to the status update

Returns: Deep clone of the updated task

Example:
  const updated = manager.updateTaskStatus('task-1', 'working');

#### addArtifact()

(taskId: string, artifact: Artifact) => Task

### PushNotificationDispatcher

#### dispatch()

- event: Task stream event to deliver

  const results = await dispatcher.dispatch('task-1', statusUpdateEvent);

  }

### CircuitBreakerError

typeof CircuitBreakerError

Error thrown when a circuit breaker rejects a call.

Example:
  try {
    await breaker.execute(() => fetch('/api'));
  } catch (err) {
    if (err instanceof CircuitBreakerError) {
      console.log('Circuit open, using fallback');
    }
  }

### CircuitBreaker

typeof CircuitBreaker

Circuit breaker for protecting against cascading failures.

Example:
  import { CircuitBreaker } from '@cleocode/lafs/circuit-breaker';
  
  const breaker = new CircuitBreaker({
    name: 'external-api',
    failureThreshold: 5,
    resetTimeout: 30000
  });
  
  try {
    const result = await breaker.execute(async () => {
      return await externalApi.call();
    });
  } catch (error) {
    if (error instanceof CircuitBreakerError) {
      console.log('Circuit breaker is open');
    }
  }

Members:

#### state

CircuitState

Current circuit state.

#### failures

number

Total failure count since last reset.

#### successes

number

Total success count since last reset.

#### lastFailureTime

Date | undefined

Timestamp of the most recent failure.

#### consecutiveSuccesses

number

Consecutive successes since the last failure.

#### totalCalls

number

Lifetime call count.

#### halfOpenCalls

number

Number of calls made while in the HALF_OPEN state.

#### resetTimer

NodeJS.Timeout | undefined

Timer handle for the scheduled OPEN-to-HALF_OPEN transition.

#### execute()

<T>(fn: () => Promise<T>) => Promise<T>

Execute a function with circuit breaker protection.

Parameters:
- fn: Async function to execute under circuit breaker protection

Returns: The result of invoking `fn`

Example:
  const result = await breaker.execute(async () => {
    return await fetch('https://api.example.com/data');
  });

#### getState()

() => CircuitState

Get the current circuit breaker state.

Returns: The current `CircuitState`

Example:
  if (breaker.getState() === 'OPEN') {
    console.log('Circuit is open, requests will be rejected');
  }

#### getMetrics()

() => CircuitBreakerMetrics

Get a snapshot of the circuit breaker's runtime metrics.

Returns: A `CircuitBreakerMetrics` snapshot

Example:
  const metrics = breaker.getMetrics();
  console.log(`State: ${metrics.state}, Failures: ${metrics.failures}`);

#### forceOpen()

() => void

Manually open the circuit breaker, rejecting all subsequent calls.

Example:
  breaker.forceOpen();
  console.log(breaker.getState()); // 'OPEN'

#### forceClose()

() => void

Manually close the circuit breaker and reset all counters.

Example:
  breaker.forceClose();
  console.log(breaker.getState()); // 'CLOSED'

#### onSuccess()

() => void

Records a successful call and may transition from HALF_OPEN to CLOSED.

#### onFailure()

() => void

Records a failed call and may trip the circuit to OPEN.

#### transitionTo()

(newState: CircuitState) => void

Transitions the circuit to the given state, resetting HALF_OPEN call count when entering HALF_OPEN.

#### shouldAttemptReset()

() => boolean

Returns `true` if enough time has elapsed since the last failure to attempt a reset.

#### scheduleReset()

() => void

Schedules a timer to transition from OPEN to HALF_OPEN after the configured reset timeout.

#### reset()

() => void

Resets all failure/success counters and clears the pending reset timer.

### CircuitBreakerRegistry

typeof CircuitBreakerRegistry

Registry for managing multiple named circuit breakers.

Example:
  const registry = new CircuitBreakerRegistry();
  
  registry.add('payment-api', {
    failureThreshold: 3,
    resetTimeout: 60000
  });
  
  const paymentBreaker = registry.get('payment-api');

Members:

#### breakers

Map<string, CircuitBreaker>

Internal map of circuit breaker name to instance.

#### add()

(name: string, config: Omit<CircuitBreakerConfig, "name">) => CircuitBreaker

Register a new circuit breaker with the given name and configuration.

Parameters:
- name: Unique name for the circuit breaker
- config: Configuration options (name is set automatically)

Returns: The newly created `CircuitBreaker`

Example:
  const breaker = registry.add('user-service', { failureThreshold: 3 });

#### get()

(name: string) => CircuitBreaker | undefined

Retrieve a circuit breaker by name.

Parameters:
- name: Name of the circuit breaker to look up

Returns: The matching `CircuitBreaker`, or `undefined` if not found

Example:
  const breaker = registry.get('payment-api');
  if (breaker) {
    await breaker.execute(() => callPaymentApi());
  }

#### getOrCreate()

(name: string, config: Omit<CircuitBreakerConfig, "name">) => CircuitBreaker

Retrieve an existing circuit breaker or create one if it does not exist.

Parameters:
- name: Name of the circuit breaker
- config: Configuration to use if a new breaker must be created

Returns: The existing or newly created `CircuitBreaker`

Example:
  const breaker = registry.getOrCreate('cache-api', { failureThreshold: 10 });

#### getAllMetrics()

() => Record<string, CircuitBreakerMetrics>

Collect metrics from all registered circuit breakers.

Returns: A record mapping breaker names to their current metrics

Example:
  const allMetrics = registry.getAllMetrics();
  for (const [name, metrics] of Object.entries(allMetrics)) {
    console.log(`${name}: ${metrics.state}`);
  }

#### resetAll()

() => void

Force-close all registered circuit breakers, resetting their counters.

Example:
  registry.resetAll();

### AgentPoller

typeof AgentPoller

AgentPoller service — polls peek endpoint AND group conversations. Deduplicates messages by ID across both sources.

Members:

#### config

AgentPollerConfig

#### handler

MessageHandler | null

#### interval

NodeJS.Timeout | null

#### seenMessageIds

Set<string>

#### running

boolean

#### onMessage()

(handler: MessageHandler) => void

Register a message handler.

#### start()

() => void

Start the polling loop.

#### stop()

() => void

Stop the polling loop.

#### status()

() => { running: boolean; seenCount: number; }

Get poller status.

#### pollCycle()

() => Promise<void>

Single poll cycle — peek + group conversations.

#### peekMessages()

() => Promise<ConduitMessage[]>

Peek for messages mentioning this agent. Delegates to transport when available.

#### pollGroupConversation()

(conversationId: string) => Promise<ConduitMessage[]>

Poll a group conversation for recent messages that  this agent. This is the fix for the group  blind spot.

#### headers()

() => Record<string, string>

Build auth headers.

### AdapterManifest

string

Semantic version of the adapter.

string

string

Provider slug used for directory lookups.

string

Relative path to the adapter's entry point module.

#### capabilities

Capability flags declared by the adapter.

Patterns used to auto-detect the provider in a project.

### SkillLibrary

SkillLibrary

Standard interface for a skill library.  Any directory or module providing skills must implement this contract. CAAMP uses it to discover, resolve, and install skills from any source.

Members:

#### version

string

Library version string.

#### libraryRoot

string

Absolute path to the library root directory.

#### skills

SkillLibraryEntry[]

All skill entries in the catalog.

#### manifest

SkillLibraryManifest

The parsed manifest.

#### listSkills()

() => string[]

List all skill names.

#### getSkill()

Get skill metadata by name.

#### getSkillPath()

(name: string) => string

Resolve absolute path to a skill's SKILL.md file.

#### getSkillDir()

(name: string) => string

Resolve absolute path to a skill's directory.

#### readSkillContent()

(name: string) => string

Read a skill's SKILL.md content as a string.

#### getCoreSkills()

() => SkillLibraryEntry[]

Get all skills where `core === true`.

#### getSkillsByCategory()

(category: SkillLibraryEntry["category"]) => SkillLibraryEntry[]

Get skills filtered by category.

#### getSkillDependencies()

(name: string) => string[]

Get direct dependency names for a skill.

#### resolveDependencyTree()

(names: string[]) => string[]

Resolve full dependency tree for a set of skill names (includes transitive deps).

#### listProfiles()

() => string[]

List available profile names.

#### getProfile()

(name: string) => SkillLibraryProfile | undefined

Get a profile definition by name.

#### resolveProfile()

#### validateSkillFrontmatter()

#### getDispatchMatrix()

### RegistryProvider

RegistryProvider

Raw provider definition as stored in registry.json before path resolution.

Members:

#### id

string

Unique provider identifier (e.g. `"claude-code"`).

#### toolName

string

Human-readable tool name (e.g. `"Claude Code"`).

#### vendor

string

Vendor/company name (e.g. `"Anthropic"`).

#### agentFlag

string

CLI flag name for `--agent` selection.

#### aliases

string[]

Alternative names that resolve to this provider.

#### pathGlobal

string

Global instruction file directory path (may contain platform variables).

#### pathProject

string

Project-relative instruction file directory path.

#### instructFile

string

Instruction file name (e.g. `"CLAUDE.md"`, `"AGENTS.md"`).

#### configKey

string

Dot-notation key path for MCP server config (e.g. `"mcpServers"`).

#### configFormat

string

Config file format identifier (e.g. `"json"`, `"jsonc"`, `"yaml"`, `"toml"`).

#### configPathGlobal

string

Global config file path (may contain platform variables).

#### configPathProject

string | null

Project-relative config file path, or `null` if unsupported.

#### pathSkills

string

Global skills directory path (may contain platform variables).

#### pathProjectSkills

string

Project-relative skills directory path.

#### detection

RegistryDetection

Detection configuration for auto-discovering this provider.

#### supportedTransports

string[]

MCP transport protocol identifiers this provider supports.

#### supportsHeaders

boolean

Whether the provider supports custom HTTP headers for remote MCP servers.

#### priority

string

Priority tier identifier (`"high"`, `"medium"`, or `"low"`).

#### status

string

Lifecycle status identifier (`"active"`, `"beta"`, `"deprecated"`, `"planned"`).

#### agentSkillsCompatible

boolean

Whether the provider is compatible with the Agent Skills standard.

#### capabilities

RegistryCapabilities | undefined

Optional provider capabilities for skills, hooks, and spawn.

### McpServerConfig

McpServerConfig

Canonical MCP server configuration.

Example:
  // Remote server
  const remote: McpServerConfig = {
    type: "http",
    url: "https://mcp.example.com/sse",
  };
  
  // Local stdio server
  const local: McpServerConfig = {
    command: "npx",
    args: ["-y", "@modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem"],
  };

Members:

#### type

TransportType | undefined

Transport type (`"stdio"`, `"sse"`, or `"http"`).

#### url

string | undefined

URL for remote MCP servers.

#### headers

Record<string, string> | undefined

HTTP headers for remote MCP servers.

#### command

string | undefined

Command to run for stdio MCP servers.

#### args

string[] | undefined

Arguments for the stdio command.

#### env

Record<string, string> | undefined

Environment variables for the stdio process.

### ParsedSource

ParsedSource

Result of parsing a source string into its typed components.

Example:
  const parsed: ParsedSource = {
    type: "github",
    value: "https://github.com/owner/repo",
    inferredName: "repo",
    owner: "owner",
    repo: "repo",
  };

Members:

#### type

SourceType

Classified source type.

#### value

string

Original or normalized source value.

#### inferredName

string

Display name inferred from the source.

#### owner

string | undefined

Repository owner (for GitHub/GitLab sources).

#### repo

string | undefined

Repository name (for GitHub/GitLab sources).

#### path

string | undefined

Path within the repository (for GitHub/GitLab sources).

#### ref

string | undefined

Git ref / branch / tag (for GitHub/GitLab sources).

SkillMetadata

Metadata extracted from a SKILL.md frontmatter.

  const meta: SkillMetadata = {
    name: "my-skill",
    description: "A useful skill for code generation",
    version: "1.0.0",
  };

### SkillEntry

SkillEntry

A discovered skill entry with its location and metadata.

Example:
  import { getCanonicalSkillsDir } from "./core/paths/standard.js";
  import { join } from "node:path";
  
  const entry: SkillEntry = {
    name: "my-skill",
    scopedName: "my-skill",
    path: join(getCanonicalSkillsDir(), "my-skill"),
    metadata: { name: "my-skill", description: "A skill" },
  };

Members:

#### name

string

Skill name.

#### scopedName

string

Scoped name (may include marketplace scope).

#### path

string

Absolute path to the skill directory.

#### metadata

SkillMetadata

Parsed SKILL.md frontmatter metadata.

#### source

string | undefined

Original source from which the skill was installed.

### LockEntry

LockEntry

A single entry in the CAAMP lock file tracking an installed skill or MCP server.

Example:
  import { getCanonicalSkillsDir } from "./core/paths/standard.js";
  import { join } from "node:path";
  
  const entry: LockEntry = {
    name: "my-skill",
    scopedName: "my-skill",
    source: "https://github.com/owner/repo",
    sourceType: "github",
    installedAt: "2025-01-15T10:30:00.000Z",
    agents: ["claude-code", "cursor"],
    canonicalPath: join(getCanonicalSkillsDir(), "my-skill"),
    isGlobal: true,
  };

Members:

#### name

string

Skill or server name.

#### scopedName

string

Scoped name (may include marketplace scope).

#### source

string

Original source string.

#### sourceType

SourceType

Classified source type.

#### version

string | undefined

Version string or commit SHA.

#### installedAt

string

ISO 8601 timestamp of first installation.

#### updatedAt

string | undefined

ISO 8601 timestamp of last update.

#### agents

string[]

Provider IDs this entry is linked to.

#### canonicalPath

string

Absolute path to canonical installation.

#### isGlobal

boolean

Whether this was installed globally.

#### projectDir

string | undefined

Project directory (for project-scoped installs).

### CaampLockFile

CaampLockFile

The CAAMP lock file structure, stored at the resolved canonical lock path.

Example:
  const lock: CaampLockFile = {
    version: 1,
    skills: {},
    mcpServers: {},
    lastSelectedAgents: ["claude-code"],
  };

Members:

#### version

1

Lock file schema version.

#### skills

Record<string, LockEntry>

Installed skills keyed by name.

#### mcpServers

Record<string, LockEntry>

Installed MCP servers keyed by name.

#### lastSelectedAgents

string[] | undefined

Last selected agent IDs for UX persistence.

### MarketplaceSkill

MarketplaceSkill

A skill listing from a marketplace search result.

Example:
  const skill: MarketplaceSkill = {
    id: "abc123",
    name: "my-skill",
    scopedName: "@author/my-skill",
    description: "A useful skill",
    author: "author",
    stars: 42,
    forks: 5,
    githubUrl: "https://github.com/author/my-skill",
    repoFullName: "author/my-skill",
    path: "/",
    hasContent: true,
  };

Members:

#### id

string

Unique marketplace identifier.

#### name

string

Skill name.

#### scopedName

string

Scoped name (e.g. `"@author/my-skill"`).

#### description

string

Short description.

#### author

string

Author / publisher name.

#### stars

number

GitHub star count.

#### forks

number

GitHub fork count.

#### githubUrl

string

GitHub repository URL.

#### repoFullName

string

Full `owner/repo` name.

#### path

string

Path within the repository.

#### category

string | undefined

Optional category tag.

#### hasContent

boolean

Whether SKILL.md content was fetched.

### MarketplaceSearchResult

MarketplaceSearchResult

Paginated search results from a marketplace API.

Example:
  const result: MarketplaceSearchResult = {
    skills: [],
    total: 0,
    limit: 20,
    offset: 0,
  };

Members:

#### skills

MarketplaceSkill[]

Array of matching skills.

#### total

number

Total number of matching results.

#### limit

number

Maximum results per page.

#### offset

number

Offset into the result set.

### AuditRule

AuditRule

A security audit rule definition with a regex pattern to match against skill content.

Example:
  const rule: AuditRule = {
    id: "SEC001",
    name: "shell-injection",
    description: "Potential shell injection vector",
    severity: "critical",
    category: "injection",
    pattern: /rm\s+-rf\s+\//,
  };

Members:

#### id

string

Unique rule identifier (e.g. `"SEC001"`).

#### name

string

Rule name.

#### description

string

Human-readable description of what the rule detects.

#### severity

AuditSeverity

Severity level of findings from this rule.

#### category

string

Category grouping (e.g. `"injection"`, `"exfiltration"`).

#### pattern

RegExp

Regex pattern to match against each line of content.

### AuditFinding

AuditFinding

A single finding from a security audit scan, with line-level location.

Example:
  const finding: AuditFinding = {
    rule: myRule,
    line: 42,
    column: 10,
    match: "rm -rf /",
    context: "Execute: rm -rf / to clean up",
  };

Members:

#### rule

AuditRule

The rule that triggered this finding.

#### line

number

Line number (1-based).

#### column

number

Column number (1-based).

#### match

string

The matched text.

#### context

string

The full line of text for context.

### AuditResult

AuditResult

Aggregate audit result for a single file.

Example:
  const result: AuditResult = {
    file: "/path/to/SKILL.md",
    findings: [],
    score: 100,
    passed: true,
  };

Members:

#### file

string

Path to the scanned file.

#### findings

AuditFinding[]

All findings for this file.

#### score

number

Security score from 0 (dangerous) to 100 (clean).

#### passed

boolean

Whether the file passed the audit (no critical/high findings).

### InjectionCheckResult

InjectionCheckResult

Result of checking a single instruction file for CAAMP injection status.

Example:
  const check: InjectionCheckResult = {
    file: "/project/CLAUDE.md",
    provider: "claude-code",
    status: "current",
    fileExists: true,
  };

Members:

#### file

string

Absolute path to the instruction file.

#### provider

string

Provider ID that owns this instruction file.

#### status

InjectionStatus

Current injection status.

#### fileExists

boolean

Whether the instruction file exists on disk.

### McpServerEntry

McpServerEntry

An MCP server entry read from a provider's config file.

Example:
  const entry: McpServerEntry = {
    name: "filesystem",
    providerId: "claude-code",
    providerName: "Claude Code",
    scope: "project",
    configPath: "/project/.claude.json",
    config: { command: "npx", args: ["-y", "@mcp/server-filesystem"] },
  };

Members:

#### name

string

Server name (the key in the config file).

#### providerId

string

Provider ID that owns this config file.

#### providerName

string

Human-readable provider name.

#### scope

"project" | "global"

Whether from project or global config.

#### configPath

string

Absolute path to the config file.

#### config

Record<string, unknown>

Raw server configuration object.

### GlobalOptions

  };

boolean | undefined

#### all

boolean | undefined

Target all detected agents.

#### json

boolean | undefined

Output as JSON.

#### dryRun

boolean | undefined

Preview changes without writing.

#### verbose

boolean | undefined

Enable debug logging.

#### quiet

boolean | undefined

Suppress non-error output.

### DetectionResult

DetectionResult

Result of detecting whether a provider is installed on the system.

Example:
  const provider = getProvider("claude-code")!;
  const result = detectProvider(provider);
  if (result.installed) {
    console.log(`Found via: ${result.methods.join(", ")}`);
  }

Members:

#### provider

Provider

The provider that was checked.

#### installed

boolean

Whether the provider was detected as installed.

#### methods

string[]

Detection methods that matched (e.g. `["binary", "directory"]`).

#### projectDetected

boolean

Whether the provider has project-level config in the current directory.

### DetectionCacheOptions

DetectionCacheOptions

Options for controlling the detection result cache.

Members:

#### forceRefresh

boolean | undefined

Whether to bypass the cache and force a fresh detection scan.

#### ttlMs

number | undefined

Time-to-live for cached results in milliseconds.

### SkillInstallResult

SkillInstallResult

Result of installing a skill to the canonical location and linking to agents.

Example:
  const result = await installSkill(sourcePath, "my-skill", providers, true);
  if (result.success) {
    console.log(`Installed to ${result.canonicalPath}`);
    console.log(`Linked to: ${result.linkedAgents.join(", ")}`);
  }

Members:

#### name

string

Skill name.

#### canonicalPath

string

Absolute path to the canonical installation directory.

#### linkedAgents

string[]

Provider IDs that were successfully linked.

#### errors

string[]

Error messages from failed link operations.

#### success

boolean

Whether at least one agent was successfully linked.

string | null

The handler execution mechanisms this provider supports.

boolean

Whether the provider's hook system is considered experimental or unstable.

Record<"ConfigChange" | "Notification" | "PermissionRequest" | "PostCompact" | "PostModel" | "PostToolUse" | "PostToolUseFailure" | "PreCompact" | "PreModel" | "PreToolUse" | "PromptSubmit" | "ResponseComplete" | "SessionEnd" | "SessionStart" | "SubagentStart" | "SubagentStop" | "ApprovalExpired" | "ApprovalGranted" | "ApprovalRequested" | "MemoryDecisionStored" | "MemoryLearningStored" | "MemoryObserved" | "MemoryPatternStored" | "PipelineManifestAppended" | "PipelineStageCompleted" | "SessionEnded" | "SessionStarted" | "TaskBlocked" | "TaskCompleted" | "TaskCreated" | "TaskStarted", HookMapping>

Mapping of every canonical event to this provider's native representation.

string[]

Native event names that exist only in this provider with no canonical equivalent.

### RecommendationScoreBreakdown

RecommendationScoreBreakdown

#### mustHave

#### prefer

number

Score contribution from preferred term matches.

#### query

number

Score contribution from query token matches.

#### stars

number

Score contribution from repository star count signal.

#### metadata

number

Score contribution from metadata quality and source confidence.

#### modernity

number

Score contribution from modern vs legacy marker detection.

#### exclusionPenalty

number

Penalty applied for matching excluded terms.

#### total

number

The final aggregated recommendation score.

number

#### excluded

boolean

Whether the skill matched one or more exclusion terms.

### RecommendationWeights

RecommendationWeights

Numeric weights controlling the recommendation scoring algorithm.

Members:

#### mustHaveMatch

number

Weight applied per must-have term match.

#### preferMatch

number

Weight applied per preferred term match.

#### queryTokenMatch

number

Weight applied per query token match.

#### starsFactor

number

Multiplier for the logarithmic star count signal.

#### metadataBoost

number

Boost for metadata quality and source confidence.

#### modernMarkerBoost

number

Boost applied per modern technology marker match.

#### legacyMarkerPenalty

number

Penalty applied per legacy technology marker match.

#### excludePenalty

number

Penalty applied per excluded term match.

#### missingMustHavePenalty

number

Penalty applied per missing must-have term.

### RecommendSkillsQueryOptions

RecommendSkillsQueryOptions

Options for the recommendation query combining ranking options with a result limit.

Members:

#### limit

number | undefined

Maximum number of results to return.

### GitFetchResult

GitFetchResult

Result of fetching a Git repository to a local temporary directory.

Members:

#### localPath

string

Absolute path to the fetched content on disk.

#### cleanup

() => Promise<void>

Cleanup function that removes the temporary directory.

### ValidationIssue

ValidationIssue

A single validation issue found during SKILL.md validation.

Example:
  const issue: ValidationIssue = {
    level: "error",
    field: "name",
    message: "Missing required field: name",
  };

Members:

#### level

"error" | "warning"

Severity: `"error"` causes validation failure, `"warning"` does not.

#### field

string

The field or section that triggered the issue.

#### message

string

Human-readable description of the issue.

### ValidationResult

ValidationResult

Result of validating a SKILL.md file against the Agent Skills standard.

Example:
  const result = await validateSkill("/path/to/SKILL.md");
  if (!result.valid) {
    for (const issue of result.issues) {
      console.log(`[${issue.level}] ${issue.field}: ${issue.message}`);
    }
  }

Members:

#### valid

boolean

Whether the skill passed validation (no error-level issues).

#### issues

ValidationIssue[]

All issues found during validation.

#### metadata

Record<string, unknown> | null

Parsed frontmatter metadata, or `null` if parsing failed.

### SpawnOptions

SpawnOptions

Options for spawning a subagent.

Members:

#### prompt

string

The prompt or instruction to give the spawned agent.

#### model

string | undefined

Model to use for the spawned agent.

#### tools

string[] | undefined

Tools to make available to the spawned agent.

#### timeout

number | undefined

Timeout in milliseconds for the spawned agent.

#### isolate

boolean | undefined

Whether to isolate the spawned agent (e.g. in a worktree).

### SpawnResult

SpawnResult

Result from a spawn operation.

Members:

#### instanceId

string

Unique identifier for the spawned agent instance.

#### status

"running" | "completed" | "failed"

Current status of the spawned agent.

#### output

string | undefined

Output produced by the spawned agent.

### SpawnAdapter

SpawnAdapter

Provider-neutral interface for spawning and managing subagents.  Concrete implementations will be provider-specific (e.g. ClaudeCodeSpawnAdapter, CodexSpawnAdapter) and registered by CLEO's orchestration layer.

Members:

#### canSpawn()

(provider: Provider) => boolean

Check if a provider supports spawning via this adapter.

#### spawn()

(provider: Provider, options: SpawnOptions) => Promise<SpawnResult>

Spawn a new subagent for the given provider.

#### listRunning()

(provider: Provider) => Promise<SpawnResult[]>

List currently running subagent instances.

#### terminate()

(provider: Provider, instanceId: string) => Promise<void>

Terminate a running subagent instance.

### UnconvertedSection

UnconvertedSection

A section of markdown that was not converted.

Members:

number

1-based line number where the section starts.

#### lineEnd

number

1-based line number where the section ends.

string

Human-readable reason why conversion was skipped.

string

The raw markdown content of the section.

### MarkdownSection

MarkdownSection

A parsed markdown section identified by heading.  Used internally by the markdown parser to structure the input before classification and conversion.

Members:

#### heading

string

The heading text (without the `#` prefix).

#### level

number

The heading level (2 for ##, 3 for ###, etc.).

#### lineStart

number

1-based line number where the section starts.

#### lineEnd

number

1-based line number where the section ends (inclusive).

#### bodyLines

string[]

Lines of content below the heading (excluding the heading line).

#### classification

SectionClassification

Classified type of this section, determined by heuristic matching.

### ExtractedProperty

ExtractedProperty

A key-value property extracted from a markdown bullet list.  Matches patterns like `- **Key**: value` or `- Key: value`.

Members:

#### key

string

Property key (lowercased, normalized).

#### value

string

Property value (trimmed).

### ExtractedPermission

ExtractedPermission

A permission entry extracted from markdown.  Matches patterns like `- Tasks: read, write` or `- Read and write tasks`.

Members:

#### domain

string

The domain (e.g. "tasks", "session", "memory").

#### values

string[]

Permission values (e.g. ["read", "write"]).

### CantDocumentIR

CantDocumentIR

Intermediate representation of a CANT document for serialization.  This is the bridge between the converter's output and the final .cant file text. Each field maps to a CANT construct.

Members:

#### kind

string

Document kind (agent, skill, hook, workflow).

#### version

number

Document version (default: 1).

#### block

CantBlockIR

The primary block (agent definition, hook, workflow, etc.).

### CantBlockIR

CantBlockIR

A CANT block (agent, skill, hook, or workflow).

Members:

#### type

string

Block type keyword (agent, skill, on, workflow, pipeline).

#### name

string

Block name/identifier (agent name, event name, workflow name).

#### properties

CantPropertyIR[]

Key-value properties.

#### permissions

ExtractedPermission[]

Permission entries (only for agent blocks).

#### children

CantBlockIR[]

Nested sub-blocks (hooks inside agents, steps in pipelines, etc.).

#### bodyLines

string[] | undefined

Raw body lines for hook/workflow bodies that are directive sequences.

### CantPropertyIR

CantPropertyIR

A single property key-value pair.

Members:

#### key

string

Property key.

#### value

string | number | boolean | string[]

Property value (string, array, number, boolean).

RcasdManifest

### PopulateEmbeddingsOptions

PopulateEmbeddingsOptions

Options for the embedding backfill pipeline.

Example:
  await populateEmbeddings(root, {
    batchSize: 25,
    onProgress: (current, total) => console.log(`${current}/${total}`),
  });

Members:

#### batchSize

number | undefined

Maximum items processed per batch cycle. Defaults to 50.

#### onProgress

((current: number, total: number) => void) | undefined

Progress callback invoked after each observation is attempted. `current` is the 1-based count of observations attempted so far; `total` is the full count of observations that need embeddings.

### ParamCliDef

ParamCliDef

CLI-specific decoration for a parameter. All fields are optional — omit the entire `cli` key for params with no CLI surface.

Members:

#### positional

boolean | undefined

When true, registers as `.argument('<name>')` (positional). When false or omitted, registers as `.option('--name <value>')`.  false

#### short

string | undefined

Short flag alias, e.g. `'-t'` for `--type`, `'-s'` for `--status`. Only meaningful when `positional` is false/omitted.

#### flag

string | undefined

Override the CLI flag name when it differs from the param's `name`. e.g. `name: 'includeArchive'` but `flag: 'include-archive'` Defaults to kebab-case of `name`.

#### variadic

boolean | undefined

For array-type params on the CLI: when true the option can be repeated. When false/omitted, the CLI accepts a single comma-separated string.  false

#### parse

((value: string) => unknown) | undefined

Custom parse function applied by Commander (e.g. `parseInt`).

### ParamDef

ParamDef

A fully-described parameter definition.  One `ParamDef` entry drives Commander: `.argument()` (positional) or `.option()` (flag).

Members:

#### name

string

Canonical camelCase parameter name (matches the key in `params` dict).

#### type

ParamType

Runtime value type. Drives JSON Schema `type` and Commander parsing.

#### required

boolean

When true, Commander registers as a positional argument (`<name>` or `[name]`).

#### description

string

Human-readable description used in Commander help text.

#### cli

ParamCliDef | undefined

CLI-specific metadata. Omit entire key if this param has no CLI surface.

#### enum

readonly string[] | undefined

JSON Schema `enum` constraint for this parameter.

#### hidden

boolean | undefined

When true, the parameter is excluded from schema generation. Use for CLI-only params (e.g. `--dry-run`, `--offset`).  false

### DispatchRequest

DispatchRequest

Canonical request shape that the CLI adapter produces.  The dispatcher validates this against the OperationRegistry before passing it through the middleware pipeline and into a DomainHandler.

Members:

#### gateway

Gateway

CQRS gateway.

#### domain

string

Target domain (canonical name).

#### operation

string

Domain-specific operation name.

#### params

Record<string, unknown> | undefined

Operation parameters (already sanitized by middleware).

#### source

"cli"

Where this request came from.

#### requestId

string

Unique request identifier for tracing.

#### sessionId

string | undefined

Bound session ID, if any.

#### _fields

string[] | undefined

LAFS field selection: filter response data to these fields only.

#### _mvi

import("/mnt/projects/cleocode/packages/lafs/dist/src/types").MVILevel | undefined

LAFS envelope verbosity. Defaults to 'standard'. 'custom' is server-set via _fields.

RateLimitMeta

#### remaining

number

#### resetMs

number

#### category

DispatchResponse

### DomainHandler

DomainHandler

Contract for domain handlers.  Each of the 9 target domains (tasks, session, memory, check, pipeline, orchestrate, tools, admin, nexus) implements this interface.

Members:

#### query()

(operation: string, params?: Record<string, unknown>) => Promise<DispatchResponse>

Execute a read-only query operation.

#### mutate()

(operation: string, params?: Record<string, unknown>) => Promise<DispatchResponse>

Execute a state-modifying mutation operation.

#### getSupportedOperations()

() => { query: string[]; mutate: string[]; }

Declared operations for introspection and validation.

### OperationDef

OperationDef

Definition of a single dispatchable operation.

Members:

#### gateway

Gateway

The CQRS gateway ('query' or 'mutate').

#### domain

"memory" | "pipeline" | "session" | "tasks" | "check" | "orchestrate" | "tools" | "admin" | "nexus" | "sticky" | "conduit"

The canonical domain this operation belongs to.

#### operation

string

The specific operation name (e.g. 'show', 'skill.list').

#### description

string

Brief description of what the operation does.

#### tier

Tier

Agent progressive-disclosure tier (0=basic, 1=memory/check, 2=full).

#### idempotent

boolean

Whether the operation is safe to retry.

#### sessionRequired

boolean

Whether this operation requires an active session.

#### requiredParams

string[]

List of parameter keys that MUST be present in the request.

#### params

ParamDef[] | undefined

Fully-described parameter list. Replaces `requiredParams` when populated. Empty array = "no declared params" (not "no params accepted"). Optional during T4897 migration — defaults to [] when absent.

Resolution

The canonical domain.

#### operation

string

The operation name.

#### def

OperationDef

The definition of the matched operation.

### ExternalTaskProvider

ExternalTaskProvider

Interface that provider adapters implement to expose their external task system to the reconciliation engine.  Provider-specific parsing lives in the adapter — core never sees native formats. Consumers implement this interface to integrate their issue tracker with CLEO.

Example:
  class LinearAdapter implements ExternalTaskProvider {
    async getExternalTasks(projectDir: string): Promise<ExternalTask[]> {
      const issues = await linearClient.issues({ projectId: '...' });
      return issues.map(issue => ({
        externalId: issue.id,
        title: issue.title,
        status: mapLinearStatus(issue.state),
        description: issue.description,
        priority: mapLinearPriority(issue.priority),
        labels: issue.labels.map(l => l.name),
        url: issue.url,
        providerMeta: { linearId: issue.identifier },
      }));
    }
  }

Members:

#### getExternalTasks()

(projectDir: string) => Promise<ExternalTask[]>

Read the provider's current task state and return normalized ExternalTasks.

Parameters:
- projectDir: Project root directory.

Returns: Array of external tasks in normalized form.

#### pushTaskState()

((tasks: ReadonlyArray<{ id: string; title: string; status: string; }>, projectDir: string) => Promise<void>) | undefined

Optionally push CLEO task state back to the provider (outbound sync). Not all providers support bidirectional sync.

Parameters:
- tasks: Current CLEO tasks to push.
- projectDir: Project root directory.

### Conduit

Conduit

The Conduit Protocol interface — high-level agent messaging.  Conduit wraps a Transport adapter, adding messaging semantics.  Implementations: - `ConduitClient` (`@cleocode/core` — wraps any Transport) - `HttpTransport` (HTTP polling to cloud SignalDock API) - `LocalTransport` (napi-rs in-process — embedded SignalDock, future) - `SseTransport` (Server-Sent Events — real-time cloud, future)  Consumers: - `@cleocode/cleo` CLI (`cleo agent watch/poll/send`) - `@cleocode/runtime` (background polling, SSE connections) - CleoOS Electron (embedded SignalDock via LocalTransport) - Agent spawners (deliver task assignments, collect results)

Members:

#### send()

(to: string, content: string, options?: ConduitSendOptions) => Promise<ConduitSendResult>

Send a message to an agent or group.

#### onMessage()

(handler: (message: ConduitMessage) => void) => ConduitUnsubscribe

Subscribe to incoming messages. Returns unsubscribe function.

#### poll()

(options?: { limit?: number; since?: string; }) => Promise<ConduitMessage[]>

One-shot poll for new messages. Returns messages without subscribing.

#### heartbeat()

() => Promise<void>

Send a heartbeat to indicate this agent is alive.

#### isOnline()

(agentId: string) => Promise<boolean>

Check if a specific agent is currently online.

#### listOnline()

(() => Promise<string[]>) | undefined

List currently online agents (optional — may not be supported by all implementations).

#### connect()

() => Promise<void>

Connect to the messaging backend.

#### disconnect()

() => Promise<void>

Disconnect from the messaging backend.

#### getState()

() => ConduitState

Get the current connection state.

#### onStateChange()

((handler: (change: ConduitStateChange) => void) => ConduitUnsubscribe) | undefined

Subscribe to connection state changes.

#### agentId

string

The agent ID this conduit instance is connected as.

### Task

Task

A single CLEO task as stored in the database.  Fields marked as required are enforced by CLEO's anti-hallucination validation at runtime. Making them required here ensures the type system catches violations at compile time rather than deferring to runtime checks.

Members:

#### id

string

Unique task identifier. Must match pattern `T\d{3,}` (e.g., T001, T5800).

#### title

string

Human-readable task title. Required, max 120 characters.

#### description

string

Task description. **Required** — CLEO's anti-hallucination rules reject tasks without a description, and require it to differ from the title.

#### status

"active" | "pending" | "cancelled" | "blocked" | "done" | "archived"

Current task status. Must be a valid `TaskStatus` enum value.

#### priority

TaskPriority

Task priority level. Defaults to `'medium'` on creation.

#### type

TaskType | undefined

Task type in hierarchy. Inferred from parent context if not specified.

#### parentId

string | null | undefined

ID of the parent task. `null` for root-level tasks.

#### position

number | null | undefined

Sort position within sibling scope.

#### positionVersion

number | undefined

Optimistic concurrency version for position changes.

#### size

TaskSize | null | undefined

Relative scope sizing (small/medium/large). NOT a time estimate.

#### phase

string | undefined

Phase slug this task belongs to.

#### files

string[] | undefined

File paths associated with this task.

#### acceptance

string[] | undefined

Acceptance criteria for completion.

#### depends

string[] | undefined

IDs of tasks this task depends on.

#### relates

TaskRelation[] | undefined

Related task entries (non-dependency relationships).

#### epicLifecycle

EpicLifecycle | null | undefined

Epic lifecycle state. Only meaningful when `type = 'epic'`.

#### noAutoComplete

boolean | null | undefined

When true, epic will not auto-complete when all children are done.

#### blockedBy

string | undefined

Reason the task is blocked (free-form text).

#### notes

string[] | undefined

Timestamped notes appended during task lifecycle.

#### labels

string[] | undefined

Classification labels for filtering and grouping.

#### origin

TaskOrigin | null | undefined

Task origin/provenance category.

#### createdAt

string

ISO 8601 timestamp of task creation. Must not be in the future.

#### updatedAt

string | null | undefined

ISO 8601 timestamp of last update. Set automatically on mutation.

#### completedAt

string | undefined

ISO 8601 timestamp of task completion. Set when `status` transitions to `'done'`. See `CompletedTask` for the status-narrowed type where this is required.

#### cancelledAt

string | undefined

ISO 8601 timestamp of task cancellation. Set when `status` transitions to `'cancelled'`. See `CancelledTask` for the status-narrowed type where this is required.

#### cancellationReason

string | undefined

Reason for cancellation. Required when `status = 'cancelled'`. See `CancelledTask` for the status-narrowed type where this is required.

#### verification

TaskVerification | null | undefined

Verification pipeline state.

#### provenance

TaskProvenance | null | undefined

Provenance tracking (who created/modified, which session).

#### pipelineStage

string | null | undefined

RCASD-IVTR+C pipeline stage this task is associated with.  Valid values: research, consensus, architecture_decision, specification, decomposition, implementation, validation, testing, release, contribution.  Auto-assigned on creation; only moves forward through stages.  T060

#### assignee

string | null | undefined

Agent ID that has claimed/is assigned to this task. Null when unclaimed.

### TaskCreate

TaskCreate

Input type for creating a new task via `addTask()`.  Only the fields the caller MUST provide are required. All other fields have sensible defaults applied by the creation logic: - `status` defaults to `'pending'` - `priority` defaults to `'medium'` - `type` is inferred from parent context - `size` defaults to `'medium'`

Members:

#### title

string

Human-readable task title. Required, max 120 characters.

#### description

string

Task description. **Required** — CLEO's anti-hallucination rules reject tasks without a description, and require it to differ from the title.

#### status

"active" | "pending" | "cancelled" | "blocked" | "done" | "archived" | undefined

Initial status. Defaults to `'pending'`.

#### priority

TaskPriority | undefined

Priority level. Defaults to `'medium'`.

#### type

TaskType | undefined

Task type. Inferred from parent context if not specified.

#### parentId

string | null | undefined

Parent task ID for hierarchy placement.

#### size

TaskSize | undefined

Relative scope sizing. Defaults to `'medium'`.

#### phase

string | undefined

Phase slug to assign. Inherited from project.currentPhase if not specified.

#### labels

string[] | undefined

Classification labels.

#### files

string[] | undefined

File paths associated with this task.

#### acceptance

string[] | undefined

Acceptance criteria.

#### depends

string[] | undefined

IDs of tasks this task depends on.

#### notes

string | undefined

Initial note to attach.

#### position

number | undefined

Sort position. Auto-calculated if not specified.
